questions.question: Explain the difference between ActionListener and DocumentListener in the context of JTextField. When would you use each?
keyPoints: ActionListener triggers only on Enter key press
DocumentListener fires on every document change (insert, remove, modify)
Use ActionListener for form submission scenarios
Use DocumentListener for real-time validation and feedback
DocumentListener has three methods: insertUpdate(), removeUpdate(), changedUpdate()

questions.question: How do you restrict a JTextField to accept only numeric input? Provide a code example using DocumentFilter.
keyPoints: Use PlainDocument as base class
Override insertString() method
Validate each character before insertion
Use regex or Character.isDigit() for validation
Reject invalid characters by not inserting them

questions.question: What is the MVC architecture in JTextField? Explain each component's role.
keyPoints: Model: PlainDocument stores text content and enforces constraints
View: UI delegate (BasicTextFieldUI) handles rendering
Controller: Event handling manages user interactions
Separation allows customizing any layer independently
Document is the key interface connecting Model and View

questions.question: Write a code snippet to create a JTextField with the following specifications: initial text "Enter Name", 20 columns, right-aligned, and read-only.
keyPoints: Use constructor: new JTextField(String, int) for text and columns
Use setHorizontalAlignment(SwingConstants.RIGHT) for alignment
Use setEditable(false) for read-only behavior
Import javax.swing.JTextField, javax.swing.SwingConstants
Optional: setForeground() for visual distinction in read-only state

questions.question: Explain the purpose of DocumentFilter in JTextField. How does it differ from KeyListener?
keyPoints: DocumentFilter intercepts document changes before application
KeyListener operates at keyboard input level
DocumentFilter handles paste operations correctly
KeyListener can be bypassed by paste; DocumentFilter cannot
DocumentFilter is the preferred approach for input validation

questions.question: What are the security considerations when using JTextField vs JPasswordField?
keyPoints: JPasswordField returns char[] instead of String
Char arrays can be cleared after use
Strings remain in memory until garbage collection
Use getPassword() for sensitive data input
Always clear password arrays after processing

questions.question: Design a complete Swing application with login functionality using JTextField for username and JPasswordField for password. Include input validation, event handling, and proper MVC separation.
keyPoints: Create JFrame as main container
Use JPanel with GridLayout for form layout
JTextField for username with setColumns(20)
JPasswordField for password with setColumns(20)
DocumentListener for real-time username validation (alphanumeric only)
ActionListener on password field for form submission
DocumentFilter for character limit on username (max 15 chars)
Display error messages using JOptionPane or JLabel
Use BorderFactory for visual separation
Proper focus management with requestFocusInWindow()
Close operation and window packing

questions.question: Explain the complete event handling mechanism in JTextField. Discuss all available listeners, their triggering conditions, and best practices for building a validated input form.
keyPoints: DocumentListener: Real-time changes - insertUpdate, removeUpdate, changedUpdate
ActionListener: Enter key press - actionPerformed
FocusListener: Focus gained/lost events
KeyListener: Low-level key events (not recommended for validation)
InputVerifier: For input validation when focus changes
Use DocumentFilter for character-level validation
Chain multiple listeners appropriately
Avoid heavy computations in listeners for performance
Use invokeLater for thread-safe UI updates
DocumentListener vs InputVerifier: immediate feedback vs focus-change validation

questions.question: Create a custom JTextField implementation that functions as a currency input field. It should: accept only numeric input with exactly 2 decimal places, display currency symbol, format number with commas, and validate on input.
keyPoints: Extend JTextField or PlainDocument
Override insertString() in custom Document
Validate: digits only, calculate decimal position
Format: use DecimalFormat for thousand separators
Prepend currency symbol ($, €, etc.) using setPrefix() concept
Handle backspace and delete operations correctly
Implement DocumentListener for formatted display update
Use regex for validation: ^\d{1,3}(,\d{3})*(\.\d{2})?$
Consider caret position after formatting
Edge cases: empty field, single digit, various decimal lengths
